title: Pointer Arithmetic Demo
description: Traverse an array using pointer incrementation.
problemStatement:
Pointers and arrays are closely linked. Use a pointer `ptr` to print all elements of the array `int arr[] = {10, 20, 30}` without using array indexing (e.g., no `arr[i]`).

**Requirement:** Use `ptr++` to move to the next element.

**Expected Output:**
`10` 
`20` 
`30`
hints: Dereference the pointer `*ptr` to get the value, then increment it.
